title: Physics-Prior Masked Multi-Stage Search for Fast-Charge Protocol Design Under Thermal Stress
note: Revised after the PSMS-v2 main result and held-out validation replaced the older UA3-centric paper story.
story: Starting from a trusted five-protocol PyBaMM baseline, the paper should show that fixed three-stage protocol search eventually saturates and that a richer staged protocol family plus feasibility-aware constrained search can push the frontier further. PSMS-v2 combines entry-aware staging, physics-prior masking, and sequence-order biases inside the same local robust search loop, yielding a stronger protocol than both the accepted baseline and the previous EVTBO anchor while staying rank-1 on a held-out combined-stress bundle. Comparable single-component ablations show that none of those added ingredients is individually decisive under the accepted four-metric contract, so the supported claim is a package-level PSMS upgrade rather than a single-mechanism story.
ten_questions: What concrete limitation of the accepted BO_3step_aggressive baseline and the later EVTBO anchor motivates moving beyond fixed three-stage search?
Why is it defensible to keep the trusted PyBaMM comparison contract while upgrading the protocol representation itself?
What package of representation and feasibility-aware search changes does PSMS-v2 add beyond EVTBO, and how should that package be described without over-claiming any one ingredient?
How is the accepted four-metric contract preserved while introducing the richer protocol family?
How did the search frontier progress from VTBO to EVTBO to PSMS-v2, and where did the largest gain appear?
How much nominal improvement does PSMS-v2 achieve over BO_3step_aggressive and over EVTBO on Q30 and the degradation metrics?
What is the final discovered protocol and how should its staged structure be interpreted physically?
Does the PSMS-v2 winner remain rank-1 on the held-out combined-stress bundle outside the search-time scenarios?
Which parts of the claim are strongly supported now, and which boundaries must remain explicit?
What figures, tables, and appendix material are needed to make the PSMS-v2 mechanism and evidence easy to verify?

detailed_outline.title: Physics-Prior Masked Multi-Stage Search for Fast-Charge Protocol Design Under Thermal Stress
detailed_outline.abstract: We study battery fast-charge protocol optimization under thermal and degradation stress using a PyBaMM-based simulation benchmark and a trusted five-protocol baseline family. Starting from the accepted BO_3step_aggressive comparator and the later EVTBO incumbent, we introduce PSMS-v2, a physics-prior masked multi-stage search package that expands the staged protocol family and uses feasibility-aware local search under the same four-metric contract. The final protocol, UA5_PSMS_3.00C_rest10m_4.30C_e4.50_v4p18_h3.35_1p5m, improves nominal Q30 from 0.08990 Ah to 0.09636 Ah over BO_3step_aggressive and from 0.09522 Ah to 0.09636 Ah over EVTBO while keeping plating loss unchanged and further reducing SEI growth and total LLI. A held-out combined-stress validation outside the search bundle keeps the PSMS-v2 winner rank-1 under both robust and nominal ordering with guard_pass_rate 1.0 and robust_utility 0.0706. Formal matched ablations of the sequence prior and entry activation fail to isolate a single decisive driver, so we present the result as a package-level improvement in protocol representation and constrained search rather than a claim about one uniquely critical module or global optimality.
research_questions: Can a richer staged protocol representation improve robust fast-charge search after fixed three-stage search begins to saturate?

Does the full PSMS-v2 package produce a measurable gain over the EVTBO anchor under the accepted four-metric contract, and do matched single-component ablations justify keeping that claim at the package level?
Do the PSMS-v2 gains remain visible on a held-out combined-stress bundle outside the search-time scenarios?

methodology.problem_setup: Battery fast-charge protocol search from 10% to 90% SOC within a 30-minute window using PyBaMM DFN simulation with LG M50 / O'Kane2022 parameters and the accepted ax-pybamm-fastcharge comparison family.
methodology.baseline_contract: Use the accepted ax-pybamm-fastcharge baseline family, with BO_3step_aggressive as the active comparator and EVTBO as the immediate pre-PSMS anchor under the same four canonical metrics Q30, plating_loss, sei_growth, and total_lli.
method_components: Physics-prior masked multi-stage protocol parameterization with explicit entry, monitor, hold, and tail roles.
Entry-aware staging, optional hold structure, and sequence-order biases that expand the candidate family beyond EVTBO while keeping the same evaluation contract.
Trust-region local search with feasibility-aware ordering so structurally risky candidates are down-weighted before expensive simulation.
Guard-based filtering with success_rate and guard_pass_rate treated as hard feasibility signals under the same followup_v1 robust bundle.
methodology.claim_boundary: The method is claimed as a local, evidence-supported protocol-representation and feasibility-aware search upgrade within the trusted PyBaMM comparison contract, not as a universal globally optimal fast-charge controller.

experimental_designs: Baseline family rerun and trust confirmation on the existing lumo GPU environment.
Voltage-aware tapered proxy search that pushed the frontier to UA4_VTBO_3.00C_rest10m_4.25C_tail2.40C.
EVTBO follow-up search that promoted UA4_EVTBO_3.00C_rest10m_4.30C_v4p16.
PSMS-v2 larger-budget search over the richer staged family plus a matched two-way ablation suite for sequence prior and entry activation.
Held-out combined-stress fixed-protocol validation comparing the PSMS-v2 winner, the EVTBO anchor, and BO_3step_aggressive.
contributions: A physics-prior masked multi-stage protocol representation that moves the search beyond the fixed three-stage family without abandoning the accepted comparison contract.

A feasibility-aware PSMS search package that combines structured staged candidates, sequence-order biasing, and hard guard filtering; matched ablations show that none of the added ingredients alone is the sole driver under the fixed contract.
Evidence that PSMS-v2 improves the accepted baseline by +7.19% in nominal Q30, improves the previous EVTBO anchor by +1.20%, and stays rank-1 on a held-out combined-stress bundle.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.note: Motivate why fixed three-stage search begins to saturate after EVTBO and why a richer staged protocol representation is the next defensible move under thermal and degradation stress. Position the paper against BO-based fast-charge search, uncertainty-aware optimization, and safe charging work without claiming a new controller class.

sections.title: Method
sections.note: Explain how PSMS-v2 extends EVTBO with a richer staged protocol family and feasibility-aware constrained search while preserving hard guard filtering and the accepted four-metric contract. Make explicit that entry activation and sequence-order bias are package ingredients, but the supported claim is the joint PSMS package rather than any single decisive component.

sections.title: Experimental Setup
sections.note: Document the accepted PyBaMM comparison contract, the followup_v1 robust bundle used for frontier search, and the heldout_combo_v1 bundle used for out-of-bundle validation.

sections.title: Main Results
sections.note: Compare BO_3step_aggressive, UA4_VTBO_3.00C_rest10m_4.25C_tail2.40C, UA4_EVTBO_3.00C_rest10m_4.30C_v4p16, and the final PSMS-v2 winner on the canonical metrics, emphasizing the +7.19% gain over baseline and the +1.20% gain over EVTBO in nominal Q30.

sections.title: Held-out Combined-Stress Validation
sections.note: Show that the final winner remains top-ranked outside the search bundle and keep the held-out scope explicit.

sections.title: Discussion and Limitations
sections.note: State exactly what is supported by the PSMS-v2 main run and the held-out bundle, and keep the boundary explicit: this is a local but defensible robustness result, not a universal optimality proof.
